title: Neighborhood Clean-Up
subtitle: Dear Mr. Collins,
text:
I am writing to let you know that our annual neighborhood clean-up event has been rescheduled to Saturday, April 26, starting at 9:00 a.m. The event will take place at Maplewood Park, near the north entrance. Gloves, bags, and refreshments will be provided. We recommend wearing comfortable clothes and sturdy shoes. If you are unable to attend, please reply to this email so we can arrange for materials to be sent to you. We hope to see you there!

Best regards,
Sarah Lin

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the email?
options: To invite Mr. Collins to a park opening
To reschedule a community clean-up event
To ask Mr. Collins to bring supplies
To announce the cancellation of an event

questions.question: What are participants advised to wear?
What should Mr. Collins do if he cannot attend?
options: Visit the north entrance on the day

title: Library Card Renewal
subtitle: Dear Ms. Yildiz,
text:
This is a reminder that your Greenfield Public Library card will expire on May 31. To renew it, please visit the library in person and bring a valid photo ID and proof of address. Alternatively, you may renew online at greenfieldlibrary.org. Renewing your card will allow you to continue borrowing books, accessing digital resources, and attending library events. Please note that renewals take up to two business days to process.

Kind regards,
Greenfield Public Library

questions.question: What is the email mainly about?
options: A reminder to renew a library card
A change in library opening hours

questions.question: What must Ms. Yildiz bring if she renews in person?
options: Her library card and a passport photo
A photo ID and proof of address
A completed renewal form and payment
Her student ID and a utility bill

questions.question: What is indicated about the renewal process?
options: It can take up to two business days

title: Internship Offer
subtitle: Dear Mr. Nakamura,
text:
Following our recent interview, I am pleased to offer you a summer internship position in our Marketing department, beginning June 2. The internship will run for twelve weeks and is based at our central office at 44 Commerce Street. Please report to Reception on your first day at 8:30 a.m. and bring your passport and an updated resume. Professor Aydin spoke highly of your analytical skills and creativity. We look forward to welcoming you.

Sincerely,
Clara Hofmann, HR Manager

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the email?
options: To inform Mr. Nakamura he has been selected for a role
To ask Mr. Nakamura to submit references

She recommended Mr. Nakamura for the internship
She is the HR Manager at the company
She will supervise Mr. Nakamura daily

title: Team Meeting
subtitle: Dear Ms. Ferreira,
text:
I am writing to inform you that the quarterly team meeting will be held on Wednesday, October 9, at 3:00 p.m. in Meeting Room A on the second floor. We will be reviewing project timelines, budget updates, and upcoming client presentations. Please come prepared with a brief summary of your current projects. As you are relatively new to the department, this will be a great opportunity to meet everyone. If you cannot attend, please notify me in advance.

Best,
Thomas Reid

To notify a colleague about a meeting

title: Office Renovation Notice
subtitle: All Staff,
text:
Please be advised that the third floor of our building will undergo renovation work from Monday, March 10 to Friday, March 14. During this period, all staff currently based on the third floor will be temporarily relocated to the fifth floor. Access to the third floor will be restricted for safety reasons. IT equipment will be moved by our facilities team on Sunday, March 9. Employees are asked to pack their personal belongings in the boxes provided before leaving on Friday, March 7.

Facilities Management

questions.question: What is the email informing staff about?
options: A permanent change to office locations
Upcoming renovation work on the third floor
New safety regulations in the building
The installation of new IT equipment

questions.question: Where will third-floor staff work during the renovation?
What are employees asked to do before March 7?
options: Collect their IT equipment from facilities
Pack their personal belongings in provided boxes

title: Gym Membership Cancellation
subtitle: Dear Mr. Okafor,
text:
We have received your request to cancel your FitLife gym membership. Your membership will remain active until the end of your current billing cycle on August 31. After this date, you will no longer have access to our facilities. Please note that any unused personal training sessions must be used before August 31, as they cannot be refunded or transferred. If you change your mind, you are welcome to reactivate your membership at any time by contacting our front desk.

Best regards,
FitLife Membership Team

options: To offer Mr. Okafor a discounted membership
To confirm the cancellation of a gym membership
To inform Mr. Okafor of new gym facilities
To request payment for outstanding fees

options: The end of the following month

questions.question: What is indicated about unused personal training sessions?
options: They can be transferred to another member
They must be used before August 31
They will be extended for one month

title: Apartment Viewing
subtitle: Dear Ms. Huang,
text:
Thank you for your enquiry regarding the two-bedroom apartment at 18 Birchwood Avenue. I am pleased to confirm a viewing appointment for Thursday, September 4, at 4:30 p.m. The apartment is located on the second floor and includes a fully equipped kitchen, two bathrooms, and a private balcony. Monthly rent is $1,450, and a two-month deposit is required upon signing. Please bring a valid photo ID and proof of income to the viewing. I look forward to meeting you.

Kind regards,
David Park, Property Agent

title: Course Registration
subtitle: Dear Mr. Santos,
text:
I am writing to confirm your registration for the Advanced Business Communication course, which begins on Tuesday, October 7. Classes are held every Tuesday and Thursday from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. in Room 204 of the Langford Learning Centre. Please ensure that you have purchased the required textbook before the first session. A list of recommended materials has been attached to this email. If you have any questions about the course, do not hesitate to contact the administration office.

Regards,
Course Administration Team

title: Live Chat
text:
Anna Berg (9:00 a.m.)
Hi everyone! Quick reminder - the client presentation is on Friday at 2:00 p.m. We need to finalize the report and the slide deck.

Carlos Diaz (9:04 a.m.)
I've finished writing the report. I'll send it to you for review by noon today.

Mia Tanaka (9:07 a.m.)
I'm still working on the slides. I'll have them ready by Thursday evening and upload them to the shared drive.

Anna Berg (9:10 a.m.)
Great. Mia, you always do such a brilliant job with the visuals. Carlos, could you also prepare a one-page summary of the key findings?

Carlos Diaz (9:13 a.m.)
Sure, no problem. I'll add that to the report document.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of Anna's first message?
options: To assign new tasks to the team
To remind the team about an upcoming deadline
To tell the team the presentation has been postponed
To ask Carlos to rewrite the report

options: She is responsible for writing the report
She will present the slides to the client
She is in charge of creating the visual content
She has already uploaded all files

questions.question: What additional task does Anna ask Carlos to complete?
options: Write a brief summary of the main findings
Send the report directly to the client

title: Group Chat
text:
Priya Nair (3:00 p.m.)
Hey, is anyone free this weekend? I was thinking we could visit the new science museum downtown. It has a great space exhibition.

James Wu (3:05 p.m.)
Sounds fun! Does it require advance booking?

Priya Nair (3:08 p.m.)
Tickets can be bought online or at the door. Online is cheaper though.

Elena Moser (3:12 p.m.)
I'll be visiting my parents in Hamburg this weekend, so I can't join. Have fun!

James Wu (3:15 p.m.)
Priya, let's meet at the main entrance at 10:30 a.m. Saturday.

Watching a film at the cinema
Going for a walk in the park

She wants the group to visit Hamburg
She recommends a museum in Hamburg
She will meet the group later in the day

They must be booked one week in advance
Buying them online is less expensive

title: Work Chat
text:
Rachel Kim (10:00 a.m.)
Team, our supplier says material costs are rising by 12% next quarter. We need to decide how to respond.

Omar Hassan (10:04 a.m.)
Passing all of it on to customers could hurt our sales. Our margins are already tight.

Rachel Kim (10:07 a.m.)
I've contacted two alternative suppliers. One has better rates, but their delivery times are slower.

Omar Hassan (10:10 a.m.)
Unreliable delivery would be worse than the cost increase. We can't miss deadlines.

Rachel Kim (10:14 a.m.)
Agreed. I'll stay with the current supplier but negotiate better payment terms.

questions.question: What is the main topic of the conversation?
options: A rise in supplier material costs

questions.question: Why does Omar advise against switching suppliers?
options: The new supplier is more expensive
The new supplier does not deliver to their area
Slower delivery could cause missed deadlines
The current supplier offers better quality

questions.question: What does Rachel decide to do?
options: Switch to the cheaper alternative supplier
Raise prices for all customers immediately
Keep the current supplier and negotiate payment terms

title: Student Chat
text:
Leo Park (7:00 p.m.)
Hey, has anyone started the history essay? It's due Friday and I haven't even chosen a topic yet.

Yara Saleh (7:05 p.m.)
I started mine yesterday. I'm writing about the Industrial Revolution's impact on urban growth.

Leo Park (7:08 p.m.)
That sounds great. I was thinking about the causes of World War I but I'm not sure where to begin.

Yara Saleh (7:11 p.m.)
Professor Chen shared some reference articles in the course folder on the university portal. Did you check?

Leo Park (7:14 p.m.)
I forgot about those! I'll look them up now. Thanks, Yara.

questions.question: Why does Leo initiate the conversation?
options: To ask Yara to share her essay
To discuss concerns about an upcoming assignment
To remind Yara about a class deadline
To suggest a topic for Yara's essay

He has not yet started working on his essay
He plans to write about urban growth
He does not need to submit the essay on Friday

questions.question: What does Yara suggest Leo do?
options: Ask Professor Chen directly for help
Check the course folder on the university portal
Submit the essay a day early

title: Project Chat
text:
Sofia Reyes (11:00 a.m.)
Hi team, we need to discuss the timeline for the new product launch. Marketing wants everything ready by the end of next month.

Kevin Lam (11:04 a.m.)
That's quite tight. The design phase alone usually takes three weeks.

Sofia Reyes (11:07 a.m.)
I know. Let's prioritise the core features and delay the optional ones for the second release.

Nadia Volkov (11:10 a.m.)
That makes sense. I can have the technical specs ready by Wednesday if that helps.

Kevin Lam (11:13 a.m.)
Great. Let's meet tomorrow morning to align on priorities before we confirm anything with Marketing.

questions.question: What problem does Sofia raise at 11:00 a.m.?
options: A budget cut for the product launch
A tight deadline set by the Marketing team
A disagreement with the design team
A delay caused by a technical issue

questions.question: What does Sofia suggest doing to meet the deadline?
options: Focusing on core features and delaying optional ones
Asking Marketing to extend the deadline

questions.question: What will the team do tomorrow morning?

title: Flatmate Chat
text:
Tom Bridges (6:00 p.m.)
Hey everyone, the kitchen tap is leaking again. Has anyone contacted the landlord yet?

Aisha Nwosu (6:05 p.m.)
I sent him a message this morning but haven't heard back. I'll try calling him tonight.

Tom Bridges (6:08 p.m.)
Thanks. In the meantime, maybe we should put a bowl under the tap so the floor doesn't get damaged.

Chloe Martin (6:12 p.m.)
Good idea. I'll do that now. Also, it might be worth keeping a record of when we reported it, just in case.

Aisha Nwosu (6:15 p.m.)
Agreed. I'll take a photo of the leak and note the date in our shared document.

questions.question: What problem does Tom raise at 6:00 p.m.?
options: The heating in the apartment is broken
The landlord has not paid the water bill
A window in the kitchen is damaged

questions.question: What does Chloe suggest doing?
options: Moving out of the apartment temporarily
Keeping a record of when the issue was reported
Asking the landlord to reduce the rent

questions.question: What will Aisha do to document the problem?
options: Write a formal letter to the landlord
Take a photo of the leak and note the date
Post about the issue on the building's notice board

title: Travel Chat
text:
Maya Cruz (2:00 p.m.)
Hey! I'm planning a trip to Lisbon next month. Has anyone been there? Any recommendations?

Finn O'Brien (2:05 p.m.)
Yes! I went last year. Definitely visit Alfama - it's the oldest district and has amazing views.

Maya Cruz (2:08 p.m.)
That sounds perfect. What about food?

Finn O'Brien (2:11 p.m.)
Try the pastel de nata - a custard tart. You'll find them everywhere but the ones in Belem are the best.

Leila Hasan (2:14 p.m.)
Also, get a transit card for the trams and metro. It's much cheaper than buying individual tickets.

questions.question: Why does Maya initiate the conversation?
options: To share photos from a recent trip to Lisbon
To ask for travel recommendations for Lisbon
To invite her friends to join her on a trip
To ask about visa requirements for Portugal

questions.question: What does Finn recommend about food?
options: Trying a local seafood restaurant in Alfama
Visiting the food market in central Lisbon
Trying pastel de nata, especially in Belem
Booking a food tour in advance

questions.question: What does Leila suggest to save money on transport?
options: Renting a bicycle for the whole trip
Walking instead of using public transport
Getting a transit card for trams and the metro

Yes, it's on the agenda. There are also topics about the roof repair and the recycling area.

Grace Kim (9:16 a.m.)
Yes - you can email your points to the building manager before Friday noon and he'll raise them at the meeting.

title: City Opens New Riverside Cycling Path
subtitle: LAKEFIELD (March 10)
text: The city of Lakefield officially opened a new 8-kilometre cycling path along the Riverside District on Saturday. The project, funded through a combination of municipal grants and local business sponsorship, aims to reduce traffic congestion and encourage sustainable transport. Mayor Diana Cross attended the ribbon-cutting ceremony and praised community volunteers who helped design the route. The path is equipped with rest areas, water stations, and bicycle repair kiosks. Residents are encouraged to use the path and share feedback on the city's website.

title: The Rise of Urban Gardening
text: Urban gardening has grown significantly in recent years, with more city residents choosing to grow their own vegetables and herbs on balconies, rooftops, and in community plots. Experts suggest that urban gardening offers numerous benefits, including reducing food costs, improving mental well-being, and fostering a sense of community. Many cities are now providing financial support and free workshops to encourage residents to take up the practice. Participants report feeling more connected to their neighbourhoods and more conscious of their dietary choices.

What are many cities doing to support urban gardening?
options: Providing free land to all residents
Building large community gardens in every district
Making urban gardening a legal requirement

title: Local Theatre Group Marks 25th Anniversary
subtitle: CRESTWOOD (May 5)
text: The Crestwood Community Theatre celebrated its 25th anniversary this weekend with a special performance of its most beloved productions. Founded in 2000 by a group of six friends, the theatre has grown to include over 80 active members from diverse backgrounds. The anniversary show was attended by more than 300 audience members, and all ticket proceeds will be donated to the local youth arts programme. Director Helena Park thanked volunteers and supporters for their dedication over the years.

title: Benefits of Regular Walking
text: Walking is one of the simplest and most accessible forms of exercise available to people of all ages. Research shows that walking for at least 30 minutes a day can improve cardiovascular health, strengthen muscles, and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. In addition to its physical benefits, walking has been shown to reduce stress and improve mood. Health experts recommend incorporating a daily walk into your routine for a more balanced and fulfilling lifestyle.

title: New Community Centre to Open in Westfield
subtitle: WESTFIELD (September 3)
text: A new community centre will open its doors to residents of Westfield next month, offering a wide range of facilities including a gymnasium, meeting rooms, a children's play area, and a cafe. The centre, which was funded through a combination of local government investment and public donations, is located at 55 Elm Road. It will be open seven days a week from 7:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. Residents are invited to attend a free open day on October 5 to explore the facilities before the official launch.

title: The Importance of Sleep
text: Sleep is essential for maintaining good physical and mental health. According to health experts, adults should aim for between seven and nine hours of sleep per night. During sleep, the body repairs tissues, strengthens the immune system, and consolidates memories. Lack of adequate sleep has been linked to a range of health problems, including increased stress levels, reduced concentration, and a higher risk of chronic illness. Experts recommend establishing a regular sleep schedule and avoiding screens before bedtime to improve sleep quality.

What do experts recommend to improve sleep quality?
options: Taking short naps during the day
Following a regular sleep schedule and avoiding screens before bed
Sleeping fewer hours but more deeply

title: Farmers Market Celebrates Ten Years
subtitle: CARTONBURG (May 22)
text: The farmers market at Briggley Square celebrates ten years of continuous operation this Saturday. The event will feature live music, cooking demonstrations, and ample local produce. Residents are encouraged to attend and support local farmers. The market will be open from 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. A special anniversary cake will be shared with visitors at noon, and local schoolchildren will perform traditional songs during the afternoon.

options: To encourage residents to buy local produce
To provide information about an anniversary celebration
To announce the restoration of Briggley Square
To describe a new entertainment venue in Cartonburg

options: An anniversary cake will be shared with visitors

title: Screen Time and Children
text: Many parents today are concerned about the amount of time their children spend in front of screens. Research suggests that excessive screen time can negatively affect children's sleep, concentration, and social skills. However, experts point out that not all screen time is equal — educational programmes and interactive activities can have positive effects on learning. Parents are advised to set clear limits on recreational screen time, encourage outdoor play, and watch programmes together with their children when possible.
